articles.headline: Seven Killed in Kathua Clashes: Four Police Officers, Three Militants Among Deceased
articles.explainer:
• Intense clashes erupted in Kathua District, Jammu and Kashmir, resulting in significant casualties on both sides.
• Four police officers bravely lost their lives while confronting a group of heavily armed militants.
• Three militants were also neutralized during the prolonged encounter, which lasted several hours.
• The incident highlights the persistent security challenges and the sacrifices made by security forces in the region.
• Reinforcements have been deployed, and a massive search operation is underway to track any remaining infiltrators.
gsSubTopics: Internal Security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Kathua District is located in the Jammu division of Jammu and Kashmir.
Jammu and Kashmir is a Union Territory of India.
India faces challenges from cross-border terrorism in J&K.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of militancy in Jammu and Kashmir and the challenges it poses to India's internal security.
Analyze the role of external state and non-state actors in fueling unrest in the region.
Evaluate the strategies employed by Indian security forces to combat terrorism and maintain peace in J&K.
Examine the socio-economic impact of prolonged militancy on the local population and government's rehabilitation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: Indian security forces conducting a search operation in a forested area of Jammu and Kashmir.
articles.trivia: The Kathua district shares its border with Punjab and Himachal Pradesh, as well as with Pakistan.

articles.headline: Eminent Gujarati Poet and Editor Harikrishna Pathak Passes Away at 86
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Gujarati poet, short story writer, and editor, Harikrishna Pathak, breathed his last at the age of 86.
• Pathak was a significant figure in modern Gujarati literature, known for his distinctive poetic style and editorial contributions.
• He served as an editor for several prominent literary magazines, fostering new talent and enriching the literary landscape.
• His works often explored themes of human emotions, social realities, and philosophical introspection.
• Pathak received numerous awards and accolades throughout his career, cementing his legacy as a literary giant.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Harikrishna Pathak was a prominent figure in Gujarati literature.
He was known as a poet, short story writer, and editor.
His contributions are significant to modern Indian literature.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of regional literature in shaping India's cultural identity and national consciousness.
Analyze the impact of literary figures like Harikrishna Pathak on the promotion and preservation of Indian languages.
Examine how literary movements and editorial initiatives contribute to the development of a vibrant literary ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of a thoughtful elderly man, Harikrishna Pathak.
articles.trivia: Gujarati is an Indo-Aryan language native to the Indian state of Gujarat and spoken predominantly by the Gujarati people.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Transparency in Electoral Funding, Directs EC to Publish All Data
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reiterated its stance on transparency in electoral funding.
• It directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure immediate and complete publication of all data related to electoral bonds, including unique alpha-numeric codes.
• The ruling aims to enhance accountability and public scrutiny of political donations, addressing concerns about anonymous funding.
• This decision is seen as a significant step towards cleaning up election finance and strengthening democratic processes.
• The court emphasized the citizens' right to information regarding the sources of political funding.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Electoral bonds were introduced in 2017 through the Finance Act.
The scheme allowed for anonymous donations to political parties.
The Supreme Court had previously struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme in February 2024.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Supreme Court's role in upholding democratic principles and ensuring transparency in electoral processes.
Discuss the implications of the electoral bond scheme on political funding and its impact on the integrity of elections in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Election Commission of India in implementing electoral reforms and ensuring compliance with judicial directives.
Suggest further reforms needed to enhance transparency and accountability in political financing in India.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: RBI Unveils Robust New Framework to Bolster Digital Payments Security
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has introduced a comprehensive new framework aimed at enhancing the security of digital payment systems.
• The framework mandates stricter compliance standards for payment service providers, including banks and fintech companies.
• Key measures include advanced encryption protocols, multi-factor authentication, and real-time fraud detection mechanisms.
• This initiative seeks to protect consumers from cyber threats and foster greater trust in India's rapidly expanding digital economy.
• The RBI emphasized continuous monitoring and evaluation of these standards to adapt to evolving cyber risks.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: RBI is India's central bank and regulatory body for financial systems.
Digital payments have seen exponential growth in India, fueled by UPI.
The new framework addresses risks like phishing, data breaches, and unauthorized transactions.
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is a security system that requires more than one method of authentication.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of digital payment security in the context of India's digital transformation and financial inclusion goals.
Discuss the challenges faced by regulatory bodies like RBI in keeping pace with rapid technological advancements in fintech.
Evaluate the potential impact of such regulatory frameworks on innovation within the fintech sector and on consumer adoption of digital payments.
Suggest measures to balance security requirements with user convenience in digital transactions.
articles.imageDescription: A person using a smartphone for digital payment with a blurred background of a city skyline.
articles.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) processed over 13.4 billion transactions in February 2024.

articles.headline: India-France Joint Naval Exercise Concludes, Reinforces Indo-Pacific Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The joint naval exercise 'Varuna' between India and France successfully concluded in the Arabian Sea.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, maritime surveillance, and anti-submarine warfare capabilities.
• It involved advanced warships, submarines, and aircraft from both navies, conducting complex tactical maneuvers.
• The collaboration underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and France, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, promoting regional stability and security.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Varuna is a bilateral naval exercise between India and France.
France is a key strategic partner for India in the Indo-Pacific.
The exercise aims to enhance maritime security and interoperability.
The Arabian Sea is part of the Indian Ocean.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-France strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how joint military exercises like 'Varuna' contribute to strengthening bilateral defense ties and regional security.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in maintaining a free and open Indo-Pacific amidst rising geopolitical tensions.
Examine the role of maritime cooperation in addressing non-traditional security threats such as piracy and illegal fishing.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French naval ships sailing in formation during a joint exercise at sea.
articles.trivia: India and France also conduct joint air exercise 'Garuda' and army exercise 'Shakti'.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype, Eyes Cost-Effective Space Access
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully testing a prototype of its Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV).
• The test involved a controlled landing and recovery demonstration, validating critical technologies for future space missions.
• This RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches by enabling repeated use of rocket components.
• The initiative positions India as a leader in developing sustainable and economical access to space.
• Further tests and development are planned to scale up the RLV for operational missions, including Gaganyaan.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator) is a previous ISRO project.
Reusable launch vehicles aim to reduce launch costs significantly.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's future space endeavors and its global standing in space exploration.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable rockets compared to conventional expendable launch systems.
Evaluate the challenges ISRO faces in perfecting RLV technology and integrating it into its operational launch schedule.
Examine how advancements in space technology like RLVs contribute to national development and security.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's impression of ISRO's reusable launch vehicle prototype descending for a controlled landing.
articles.trivia: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is currently the world's only orbital-class reusable rocket.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Development' 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled the 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Development 2025'.
• The policy aims to foster green infrastructure, efficient resource management, and resilient urban planning across Indian cities.
• Key pillars include promoting renewable energy, improving waste management, enhancing public transport, and conserving urban biodiversity.
• It emphasizes citizen participation and the adoption of smart city solutions for better governance and quality of life.
• The policy sets ambitious targets for reducing carbon footprint and achieving climate resilience in urban areas by 2035.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry for urban development.
Sustainable Urban Development focuses on environmental, social, and economic sustainability.
Smart Cities Mission is an existing government initiative for urban transformation.
India is committed to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical components of the National Policy on Sustainable Urban Development and its potential to transform Indian cities.
Discuss the challenges in implementing sustainable urban policies, including funding, governance, and public awareness.
Evaluate the role of technology and citizen participation in achieving the goals of sustainable urban development.
Examine how this policy aligns with India's international commitments on climate change and sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic green city with vertical gardens, solar panels, and integrated public transport.
articles.trivia: By 2050, it is estimated that over 50% of India's population will live in urban areas.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Poshan Abhiyan 2.0' to Intensify Fight Against Malnutrition
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has launched 'Poshan Abhiyan 2.0', an enhanced version of the National Nutrition Mission.
• The revamped program integrates various nutrition-related schemes to create a comprehensive strategy for tackling malnutrition.
• It focuses on the first 1,000 days of a child's life, adolescent girls, and pregnant and lactating mothers.
• Key features include improved delivery mechanisms, use of technology for monitoring, and community-based interventions.
• The initiative aims for a 'Malnutrition-Free India' by 2030, aligning with Sustainable Development Goal 2 (Zero Hunger).
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018.
The Ministry of Women and Child Development is the nodal ministry.
It aims to reduce stunting, undernutrition, anemia, and low birth weight.
The first 1000 days are crucial for a child's development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges of malnutrition in India and how Poshan Abhiyan 2.0 seeks to address them.
Analyze the importance of an integrated approach and community participation in achieving nutrition security.
Evaluate the role of technology and data in effective monitoring and delivery of welfare schemes like Poshan Abhiyan.
Examine the linkages between malnutrition, gender inequality, and socio-economic development in India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a healthy child surrounded by nutritious food items and the 'Poshan Abhiyan' logo.
articles.trivia: India accounts for a significant portion of the world's stunted children.

articles.headline: India Pledges Significant Contribution to Global Climate Resilience Fund at UN Meet
articles.explainer:
• India announced a substantial financial pledge towards the newly established Global Climate Resilience Fund during a key UN climate summit.
• The fund aims to support developing nations in adapting to the impacts of climate change and building resilience against extreme weather events.
• India's contribution underscores its commitment to global climate action and its role as a responsible developing economy.
• The announcement was made amidst discussions on climate finance and the need for greater support for vulnerable countries.
• This move reinforces India's 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (The world is one family) philosophy in addressing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: The UN Climate Summits (COPs) are held annually.
Climate resilience refers to the ability to anticipate, prepare for, and respond to hazardous events.
Developing countries often bear a disproportionate burden of climate change impacts.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving role in global climate negotiations and its commitment to both mitigation and adaptation efforts.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance for developing countries and the debate surrounding 'loss and damage'.
Evaluate the significance of international cooperation and multilateral funds in addressing the global climate crisis.
Examine how India's domestic climate policies align with its international pledges and contribute to global sustainability goals.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from various countries attending a UN climate summit, with a world map in the background.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Loss and Damage' in climate change refers to the irreversible harm caused by climate impacts.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Export Promotion Scheme for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched a dedicated 'MSME Export Promotion Scheme' to boost their global outreach.
• The scheme offers financial incentives, capacity building, and market access support to MSMEs looking to export their products.
• It aims to integrate Indian MSMEs into global value chains and increase their contribution to India's total exports.
• Key components include subsidies for international trade fairs, quality certification, and digital marketing platforms.
• This initiative is expected to create new employment opportunities and strengthen India's position in global trade.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: MSMEs are defined based on investment in plant and machinery/equipment and turnover.
The Ministry of MSME and Ministry of Commerce and Industry are involved in MSME promotion.
Exports contribute significantly to India's GDP and foreign exchange reserves.
India aims to become a global manufacturing hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of MSMEs in India's economic growth, employment generation, and export performance.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian MSMEs in accessing global markets and how the new scheme addresses them.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in promoting MSME exports and integrating them into global supply chains.
Suggest additional measures to enhance the competitiveness and resilience of India's MSME sector.
articles.imageDescription: A small factory worker inspecting products, with shipping containers in the background.
articles.trivia: MSMEs account for nearly 30% of India's GDP and 45% of its manufacturing output.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Mega Railway Electrification Project Worth ₹50,000 Crore
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to a massive railway electrification project valued at ₹50,000 crore.
• The project aims to electrify an additional 10,000 route kilometers of Indian Railways network by 2030.
• This initiative is a major step towards reducing carbon emissions, enhancing energy efficiency, and modernizing rail infrastructure.
• It will facilitate faster and more reliable train operations, benefiting both freight and passenger services.
• The project is expected to generate significant employment opportunities and boost the domestic manufacturing sector.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Indian Railways is one of the world's largest railway networks.
Electrification aims to reduce dependence on fossil fuels.
The project aligns with India's net-zero targets.
Union Cabinet is the highest decision-making body in the executive branch.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic and environmental benefits of large-scale railway electrification projects in India.
Analyze the challenges in executing mega infrastructure projects, including land acquisition, funding, and technological integration.
Evaluate the role of Indian Railways in national development and its contribution to achieving sustainable development goals.
Examine how modernization of railway infrastructure can enhance India's logistics capabilities and boost economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: An electric locomotive pulling a passenger train through a scenic Indian landscape.
articles.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world by size.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Relics in Bihar
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant ancient Buddhist relics during excavations in Bihar.
• The findings include well-preserved stupa remnants, monastic complexes, and artifacts dating back to the Gupta period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism and the cultural heritage of ancient Magadha.
• Experts believe the site could be a previously unknown major Buddhist center, potentially enhancing India's cultural tourism.
• Further excavation and research are underway to fully ascertain the historical and archaeological importance of the site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: Bihar was the heartland of ancient Magadha, where Buddhism originated.
The Gupta period (c. 320 to 550 CE) is considered the 'Golden Age' of India.
ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Nalanda and Bodh Gaya are famous Buddhist sites in Bihar.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural evolution.
Analyze the contribution of Buddhism to India's art, architecture, and philosophical traditions during the Gupta period.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and managing India's vast cultural heritage.
Examine the potential of such discoveries to boost cultural tourism and local economies in regions like Bihar.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists meticulously excavating an ancient site, revealing stone structures and artifacts.
articles.trivia: Bihar is home to Bodh Gaya, where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment and became the Buddha.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues High-Alert Cybersecurity Advisory Amidst Surge in Phishing Attacks
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued a high-priority cybersecurity advisory.
• The advisory warns against a significant surge in sophisticated phishing and ransomware attacks targeting Indian users and organizations.
• It provides detailed recommendations for individuals and enterprises to safeguard their digital assets and sensitive information.
• CERT-In emphasized the importance of strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, and regular software updates.
• The agency urged immediate reporting of any suspicious activity to mitigate potential financial losses and data breaches.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Phishing is a type of cyber attack where malicious actors impersonate trusted entities.
Ransomware encrypts data and demands payment for its release.
The Information Technology Act, 2000, governs cybercrime in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India, particularly phishing and ransomware, and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the role of government agencies like CERT-In in enhancing India's cybersecurity posture and protecting critical infrastructure.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current cybersecurity policies and legal frameworks in combating cybercrime.
Suggest measures to improve public awareness and digital literacy to mitigate the risks of cyber attacks.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a lock icon over a circuit board, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

articles.headline: New Agricultural Drone Policy to Boost Productivity and Address Labor Shortages
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new policy promoting the use of drones in agriculture.
• The policy aims to enhance farm productivity, optimize resource utilization, and address the growing challenge of labor shortages.
• Drones will be utilized for precision spraying of fertilizers and pesticides, crop health monitoring, and land surveying.
• The government will provide subsidies and regulatory support for farmers and Agri-startups adopting drone technology.
• This initiative is part of a broader push to modernize Indian agriculture and improve farmers' income.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Drones are Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs).
Precision agriculture uses technology to optimize crop yields and resource use.
The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is the nodal ministry.
India's drone rules were liberalized in 2021.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of drone technology to revolutionize Indian agriculture and address persistent challenges like low productivity and labor scarcity.
Analyze the policy framework supporting drone adoption in agriculture, including subsidies and regulatory aspects.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of agricultural drones on small and marginal farmers, and potential issues like digital divide.
Examine how integrating technology into farming aligns with the goal of doubling farmers' income and achieving food security.
articles.imageDescription: An agricultural drone spraying pesticides over a field of crops.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms for Electoral Processes Ahead of 2029 Elections
articles.explainer:
• A high-level Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending comprehensive reforms for India's electoral processes.
• The recommendations include measures to curb misuse of money power, enhance voter participation, and strengthen the Election Commission's autonomy.
• Key proposals involve stricter penalties for electoral malpractices, digital voter registration, and remote voting pilot projects.
• The panel emphasized the need for a robust and transparent electoral system to uphold the integrity of democratic elections.
• The report will now be considered by the government for legislative action ahead of the next general elections.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees are permanent and regular committees.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) conducts elections for Parliament, State Legislatures, and President/VP.
The Representation of the People Act, 1950 and 1951, govern elections.
Remote voting aims to facilitate voting for migrant workers and NRIs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges in ensuring free and fair elections in India and how the proposed electoral reforms aim to address them.
Analyze the role of parliamentary committees in legislative scrutiny and policy formulation, with specific reference to electoral reforms.
Evaluate the implications of digital voter registration and remote voting on voter accessibility and the integrity of the electoral process.
Examine the constitutional and legal framework governing elections in India and suggest further improvements.
articles.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India building at dusk.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest electorate, with over 900 million registered voters.

articles.headline: India Champions 'One Health' Approach in G20 Health Ministers' Declaration
articles.explainer:
• India played a pivotal role in drafting the G20 Health Ministers' Declaration, emphasizing the 'One Health' approach.
• The declaration calls for integrated strategies to address human, animal, and environmental health interdependencies.
• Key focus areas include pandemic preparedness, antimicrobial resistance (AMR), and strengthening health systems globally.
• India highlighted its experiences from the COVID-19 pandemic and its commitment to equitable access to vaccines and therapeutics.
• The 'One Health' framework is seen as crucial for preventing future pandemics and achieving global health security.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The 'One Health' approach recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) is a global health threat.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the 'One Health' approach in addressing complex global health challenges like pandemics and antimicrobial resistance.
Analyze India's leadership role in global health diplomacy and its contributions to international health initiatives.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a 'One Health' framework, including inter-sectoral coordination and resource mobilization.
Examine how strengthening health systems and promoting vaccine equity are crucial for achieving global health security.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health in a 'One Health' concept.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: India Inaugurates Asia's Largest Floating Solar Energy Park in Gujarat
articles.explainer:
• India has inaugurated Asia's largest floating solar energy park, located on the Sardar Sarovar Dam reservoir in Gujarat.
• The mega project boasts a capacity of 600 MW, significantly boosting India's renewable energy generation.
• Floating solar panels offer advantages like reduced land use, lower evaporation rates, and higher efficiency due to water cooling.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and combating climate change.
• The project is expected to inspire similar installations across the country, leveraging India's vast water bodies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Sardar Sarovar Dam is on the Narmada River in Gujarat.
Floating solar panels are an emerging technology for renewable energy.
India aims to achieve 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
Gujarat is a leading state in renewable energy generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the advantages and challenges of floating solar energy parks compared to conventional ground-mounted solar installations.
Analyze the role of renewable energy projects like this in India's energy transition and its commitment to climate action.
Evaluate the technological advancements and policy support needed to scale up floating solar capacity across the country.
Examine the environmental implications of large-scale floating solar projects on aquatic ecosystems and water quality.
articles.imageDescription: Vast arrays of solar panels floating on a large reservoir, reflecting the sky.
articles.trivia: The world's largest floating solar plant is currently in China.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National Data Governance Framework Policy to Streamline Public Data Access
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Data Governance Framework Policy.
• The policy aims to standardize data collection, storage, and access procedures for public sector data across India.
• It seeks to enhance data quality, promote data-driven governance, and facilitate data sharing among government entities.
• The framework also emphasizes data anonymization and privacy protection, ensuring responsible use of citizen data.
• This initiative is crucial for realizing the vision of a 'Digital India' and leveraging data for public welfare.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT policy and e-governance.
Data governance ensures effective and responsible use of data.
Data anonymization removes personally identifiable information.
The Digital India program aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a robust National Data Governance Framework in promoting data-driven governance and public service delivery.
Analyze the challenges involved in standardizing public data across various government departments and ensuring interoperability.
Evaluate the balance between data sharing for public welfare and protecting individual data privacy and security.
Examine how this policy can foster innovation and economic growth by enabling access to high-quality public data.
articles.imageDescription: A network of interconnected data points and icons representing data flow and security.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest and fastest-growing digital consumer markets in the world.

highlights: Seven Killed in Kathua Clashes: Four Police Officers, Three Militants Among Deceased, raising internal security concerns.
Supreme Court Reinforces Transparency in Electoral Funding, Directs EC to Publish All Electoral Bond Data.
ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype, Paving Way for Cost-Effective Space Access.
Government Launches 'Poshan Abhiyan 2.0' to Intensify Fight Against Malnutrition, Targeting Malnutrition-Free India by 2030.
India Inaugurates Asia's Largest Floating Solar Energy Park in Gujarat, Boosting Renewable Energy Capacity.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent clashes in Kathua District, consider the following statements:
1. Kathua District is located in the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir.
2. The clashes involved security forces and militants, resulting in casualties on both sides.
3. Jammu and Kashmir is currently a Union Territory with a legislature.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Kathua District is located in the Jammu division, not the Kashmir Valley. Statement 2 is correct, as per the news. Statement 3 is correct; Jammu and Kashmir was reorganized into a Union Territory with a legislature in 2019.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements best describes the 'One Health' approach, as emphasized by India in the G20 Health Ministers' Declaration?
(a) It focuses solely on human health outcomes through robust healthcare systems.
(b) It recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
(c) It primarily deals with the economic aspects of health and disease prevention.
(d) It is an initiative by the World Health Organization exclusively for developing countries.
options: (a) It focuses solely on human health outcomes through robust healthcare systems.
(b) It recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
(c) It primarily deals with the economic aspects of health and disease prevention.
(d) It is an initiative by the World Health Organization exclusively for developing countries.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'One Health' approach is a collaborative, multi-sectoral, and trans-disciplinary approach with the goal of achieving optimal health outcomes by recognizing the interconnection between people, animals, plants, and their shared environment. Options (a), (c), and (d) are either too narrow or incorrect.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Reusable Launch Vehicles (RLVs) in space technology:
1. RLVs aim to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
2. ISRO has successfully tested a prototype for its Next-Generation RLV.
3. RLVs are primarily designed for deep-space exploration missions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; reducing launch costs is a primary goal of RLVs. Statement 2 is correct, as reported. Statement 3 is incorrect; while RLVs can support various missions, their primary advantage is cost-effective access to Earth orbit for satellite deployment and crewed missions, not exclusively deep-space exploration.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Development 2025', which of the following are its key objectives?
1. Promoting green infrastructure and efficient resource management.
2. Enhancing public transport and conserving urban biodiversity.
3. Prioritizing vertical growth over horizontal expansion in all cities.
4. Achieving climate resilience in urban areas.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 4 are correct objectives of sustainable urban development. Statement 3, while a common urban planning strategy, is not explicitly stated as a universal priority in the policy's broad objectives, which focus more on sustainability principles rather than a single growth model for all cities. The policy promotes efficient resource management, which can include both vertical and horizontal sustainable growth depending on context.

prelimsMocks.question: Harikrishna Pathak, who recently passed away, was a prominent personality in the field of:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Harikrishna Pathak was an eminent Gujarati poet, short story writer, and editor. He was a significant figure in modern Gujarati literature.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Kathua clashes underscore the persistent security challenges in Jammu and Kashmir. Analyze the evolving nature of internal security threats in the region and critically evaluate the strategies adopted by the Indian state to counter them. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; Internal Security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Kathua incident. Discuss evolving threats (hybrid terrorism, drone infiltration, radicalization, cross-border support). Evaluate strategies (multi-layered security, intelligence, development, community engagement, diplomatic pressure). Conclude with challenges and way forward.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's directives on electoral bond transparency are a significant step towards strengthening democratic accountability. Discuss the implications of these rulings on political funding in India and suggest further reforms needed to ensure free and fair elections. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these; Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce SC ruling on electoral bonds. Discuss implications: increased transparency, accountability, citizen's right to know, potential impact on corporate funding. Suggest reforms: cap on individual/corporate donations, state funding, stricter EC powers, simplified compliance. Conclude on strengthening democratic ethos.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'One Health' approach in the G20 Health Ministers' Declaration reflects a comprehensive strategy for global health security. Elucidate the core principles of 'One Health' and analyze its significance in addressing contemporary global health challenges, including future pandemics and antimicrobial resistance. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate; Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Define 'One Health' (interconnectedness of human, animal, environment). Explain its core principles (collaboration, multisectoral, trans-disciplinary). Discuss significance for pandemics (zoonotic origins), AMR (shared environment), food safety, climate change. Conclude on its holistic and preventive nature.

quickBytes.text: Kathua District is located in the Jammu division of Jammu and Kashmir, bordering Punjab and Himachal Pradesh.
Harikrishna Pathak was a renowned Gujarati poet, short story writer, and editor.
The Supreme Court had previously struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme in February 2024.
RBI is India's central bank and regulatory body for the financial system.
Varuna is a bilateral naval exercise conducted between India and France.
ISRO's Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
Poshan Abhiyan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018 to combat malnutrition.
The 'One Health' approach recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
MSMEs account for nearly 30% of India's GDP and 45% of its manufacturing output.
CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents in India.

GS1.headline: Eminent Gujarati Poet and Editor Harikrishna Pathak Passes Away at 86
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Gujarati poet, short story writer, and editor, Harikrishna Pathak, breathed his last at the age of 86.
• Pathak was a significant figure in modern Gujarati literature, known for his distinctive poetic style and editorial contributions.
• He served as an editor for several prominent literary magazines, fostering new talent and enriching the literary landscape.
• His works often explored themes of human emotions, social realities, and philosophical introspection.
• Pathak received numerous awards and accolades throughout his career, cementing his legacy as a literary giant.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Harikrishna Pathak was a prominent figure in Gujarati literature.
He was known as a poet, short story writer, and editor.
His contributions are significant to modern Indian literature.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of regional literature in shaping India's cultural identity and national consciousness.
Analyze the impact of literary figures like Harikrishna Pathak on the promotion and preservation of Indian languages.
Examine how literary movements and editorial initiatives contribute to the development of a vibrant literary ecosystem.
GS1.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of a thoughtful elderly man, Harikrishna Pathak.
GS1.trivia: Gujarati is an Indo-Aryan language native to the Indian state of Gujarat and spoken predominantly by the Gujarati people.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Relics in Bihar
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant ancient Buddhist relics during excavations in Bihar.
• The findings include well-preserved stupa remnants, monastic complexes, and artifacts dating back to the Gupta period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism and the cultural heritage of ancient Magadha.
• Experts believe the site could be a previously unknown major Buddhist center, potentially enhancing India's cultural tourism.
• Further excavation and research are underway to fully ascertain the historical and archaeological importance of the site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: Bihar was the heartland of ancient Magadha, where Buddhism originated.
The Gupta period (c. 320 to 550 CE) is considered the 'Golden Age' of India.
ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Nalanda and Bodh Gaya are famous Buddhist sites in Bihar.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural evolution.
Analyze the contribution of Buddhism to India's art, architecture, and philosophical traditions during the Gupta period.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and managing India's vast cultural heritage.
Examine the potential of such discoveries to boost cultural tourism and local economies in regions like Bihar.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists meticulously excavating an ancient site, revealing stone structures and artifacts.
GS1.trivia: Bihar is home to Bodh Gaya, where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment and became the Buddha.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Transparency in Electoral Funding, Directs EC to Publish All Data
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reiterated its stance on transparency in electoral funding.
• It directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure immediate and complete publication of all data related to electoral bonds, including unique alpha-numeric codes.
• The ruling aims to enhance accountability and public scrutiny of political donations, addressing concerns about anonymous funding.
• This decision is seen as a significant step towards cleaning up election finance and strengthening democratic processes.
• The court emphasized the citizens' right to information regarding the sources of political funding.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Electoral bonds were introduced in 2017 through the Finance Act.
The scheme allowed for anonymous donations to political parties.
The Supreme Court had previously struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme in February 2024.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Supreme Court's role in upholding democratic principles and ensuring transparency in electoral processes.
Discuss the implications of the electoral bond scheme on political funding and its impact on the integrity of elections in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Election Commission of India in implementing electoral reforms and ensuring compliance with judicial directives.
Suggest further reforms needed to enhance transparency and accountability in political financing in India.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

GS2.headline: India-France Joint Naval Exercise Concludes, Reinforces Indo-Pacific Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The joint naval exercise 'Varuna' between India and France successfully concluded in the Arabian Sea.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, maritime surveillance, and anti-submarine warfare capabilities.
• It involved advanced warships, submarines, and aircraft from both navies, conducting complex tactical maneuvers.
• The collaboration underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and France, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, promoting regional stability and security.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Varuna is a bilateral naval exercise between India and France.
France is a key strategic partner for India in the Indo-Pacific.
The exercise aims to enhance maritime security and interoperability.
The Arabian Sea is part of the Indian Ocean.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-France strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how joint military exercises like 'Varuna' contribute to strengthening bilateral defense ties and regional security.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in maintaining a free and open Indo-Pacific amidst rising geopolitical tensions.
Examine the role of maritime cooperation in addressing non-traditional security threats such as piracy and illegal fishing.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and French naval ships sailing in formation during a joint exercise at sea.
GS2.trivia: India and France also conduct joint air exercise 'Garuda' and army exercise 'Shakti'.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Poshan Abhiyan 2.0' to Intensify Fight Against Malnutrition
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has launched 'Poshan Abhiyan 2.0', an enhanced version of the National Nutrition Mission.
• The revamped program integrates various nutrition-related schemes to create a comprehensive strategy for tackling malnutrition.
• It focuses on the first 1,000 days of a child's life, adolescent girls, and pregnant and lactating mothers.
• Key features include improved delivery mechanisms, use of technology for monitoring, and community-based interventions.
• The initiative aims for a 'Malnutrition-Free India' by 2030, aligning with Sustainable Development Goal 2 (Zero Hunger).
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018.
The Ministry of Women and Child Development is the nodal ministry.
It aims to reduce stunting, undernutrition, anemia, and low birth weight.
The first 1000 days are crucial for a child's development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges of malnutrition in India and how Poshan Abhiyan 2.0 seeks to address them.
Analyze the importance of an integrated approach and community participation in achieving nutrition security.
Evaluate the role of technology and data in effective monitoring and delivery of welfare schemes like Poshan Abhiyan.
Examine the linkages between malnutrition, gender inequality, and socio-economic development in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a healthy child surrounded by nutritious food items and the 'Poshan Abhiyan' logo.
GS2.trivia: India accounts for a significant portion of the world's stunted children.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Significant Contribution to Global Climate Resilience Fund at UN Meet
GS2.explainer:
• India announced a substantial financial pledge towards the newly established Global Climate Resilience Fund during a key UN climate summit.
• The fund aims to support developing nations in adapting to the impacts of climate change and building resilience against extreme weather events.
• India's contribution underscores its commitment to global climate action and its role as a responsible developing economy.
• The announcement was made amidst discussions on climate finance and the need for greater support for vulnerable countries.
• This move reinforces India's 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (The world is one family) philosophy in addressing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: The UN Climate Summits (COPs) are held annually.
Climate resilience refers to the ability to anticipate, prepare for, and respond to hazardous events.
Developing countries often bear a disproportionate burden of climate change impacts.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving role in global climate negotiations and its commitment to both mitigation and adaptation efforts.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance for developing countries and the debate surrounding 'loss and damage'.
Evaluate the significance of international cooperation and multilateral funds in addressing the global climate crisis.
Examine how India's domestic climate policies align with its international pledges and contribute to global sustainability goals.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from various countries attending a UN climate summit, with a world map in the background.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Loss and Damage' in climate change refers to the irreversible harm caused by climate impacts.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms for Electoral Processes Ahead of 2029 Elections
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending comprehensive reforms for India's electoral processes.
• The recommendations include measures to curb misuse of money power, enhance voter participation, and strengthen the Election Commission's autonomy.
• Key proposals involve stricter penalties for electoral malpractices, digital voter registration, and remote voting pilot projects.
• The panel emphasized the need for a robust and transparent electoral system to uphold the integrity of democratic elections.
• The report will now be considered by the government for legislative action ahead of the next general elections.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees are permanent and regular committees.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) conducts elections for Parliament, State Legislatures, and President/VP.
The Representation of the People Act, 1950 and 1951, govern elections.
Remote voting aims to facilitate voting for migrant workers and NRIs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges in ensuring free and fair elections in India and how the proposed electoral reforms aim to address them.
Analyze the role of parliamentary committees in legislative scrutiny and policy formulation, with specific reference to electoral reforms.
Evaluate the implications of digital voter registration and remote voting on voter accessibility and the integrity of the electoral process.
Examine the constitutional and legal framework governing elections in India and suggest further improvements.
GS2.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India building at dusk.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's largest electorate, with over 900 million registered voters.

GS2.headline: India Champions 'One Health' Approach in G20 Health Ministers' Declaration
GS2.explainer:
• India played a pivotal role in drafting the G20 Health Ministers' Declaration, emphasizing the 'One Health' approach.
• The declaration calls for integrated strategies to address human, animal, and environmental health interdependencies.
• Key focus areas include pandemic preparedness, antimicrobial resistance (AMR), and strengthening health systems globally.
• India highlighted its experiences from the COVID-19 pandemic and its commitment to equitable access to vaccines and therapeutics.
• The 'One Health' framework is seen as crucial for preventing future pandemics and achieving global health security.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The 'One Health' approach recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) is a global health threat.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the 'One Health' approach in addressing complex global health challenges like pandemics and antimicrobial resistance.
Analyze India's leadership role in global health diplomacy and its contributions to international health initiatives.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a 'One Health' framework, including inter-sectoral coordination and resource mobilization.
Examine how strengthening health systems and promoting vaccine equity are crucial for achieving global health security.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health in a 'One Health' concept.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils National Data Governance Framework Policy to Streamline Public Data Access
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Data Governance Framework Policy.
• The policy aims to standardize data collection, storage, and access procedures for public sector data across India.
• It seeks to enhance data quality, promote data-driven governance, and facilitate data sharing among government entities.
• The framework also emphasizes data anonymization and privacy protection, ensuring responsible use of citizen data.
• This initiative is crucial for realizing the vision of a 'Digital India' and leveraging data for public welfare.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT policy and e-governance.
Data governance ensures effective and responsible use of data.
Data anonymization removes personally identifiable information.
The Digital India program aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a robust National Data Governance Framework in promoting data-driven governance and public service delivery.
Analyze the challenges involved in standardizing public data across various government departments and ensuring interoperability.
Evaluate the balance between data sharing for public welfare and protecting individual data privacy and security.
Examine how this policy can foster innovation and economic growth by enabling access to high-quality public data.
GS2.imageDescription: A network of interconnected data points and icons representing data flow and security.
GS2.trivia: India is one of the largest and fastest-growing digital consumer markets in the world.

GS3.headline: Seven Killed in Kathua Clashes: Four Police Officers, Three Militants Among Deceased
GS3.explainer:
• Intense clashes erupted in Kathua District, Jammu and Kashmir, resulting in significant casualties on both sides.
• Four police officers bravely lost their lives while confronting a group of heavily armed militants.
• Three militants were also neutralized during the prolonged encounter, which lasted several hours.
• The incident highlights the persistent security challenges and the sacrifices made by security forces in the region.
• Reinforcements have been deployed, and a massive search operation is underway to track any remaining infiltrators.
gsSubTopics: Internal Security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Kathua District is located in the Jammu division of Jammu and Kashmir.
Jammu and Kashmir is a Union Territory of India.
India faces challenges from cross-border terrorism in J&K.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of militancy in Jammu and Kashmir and the challenges it poses to India's internal security.
Analyze the role of external state and non-state actors in fueling unrest in the region.
Evaluate the strategies employed by Indian security forces to combat terrorism and maintain peace in J&K.
Examine the socio-economic impact of prolonged militancy on the local population and government's rehabilitation efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian security forces conducting a search operation in a forested area of Jammu and Kashmir.
GS3.trivia: The Kathua district shares its border with Punjab and Himachal Pradesh, as well as with Pakistan.

GS3.headline: RBI Unveils Robust New Framework to Bolster Digital Payments Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has introduced a comprehensive new framework aimed at enhancing the security of digital payment systems.
• The framework mandates stricter compliance standards for payment service providers, including banks and fintech companies.
• Key measures include advanced encryption protocols, multi-factor authentication, and real-time fraud detection mechanisms.
• This initiative seeks to protect consumers from cyber threats and foster greater trust in India's rapidly expanding digital economy.
• The RBI emphasized continuous monitoring and evaluation of these standards to adapt to evolving cyber risks.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: RBI is India's central bank and regulatory body for financial systems.
Digital payments have seen exponential growth in India, fueled by UPI.
The new framework addresses risks like phishing, data breaches, and unauthorized transactions.
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is a security system that requires more than one method of authentication.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of digital payment security in the context of India's digital transformation and financial inclusion goals.
Discuss the challenges faced by regulatory bodies like RBI in keeping pace with rapid technological advancements in fintech.
Evaluate the potential impact of such regulatory frameworks on innovation within the fintech sector and on consumer adoption of digital payments.
Suggest measures to balance security requirements with user convenience in digital transactions.
GS3.imageDescription: A person using a smartphone for digital payment with a blurred background of a city skyline.
GS3.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) processed over 13.4 billion transactions in February 2024.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype, Eyes Cost-Effective Space Access
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully testing a prototype of its Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV).
• The test involved a controlled landing and recovery demonstration, validating critical technologies for future space missions.
• This RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches by enabling repeated use of rocket components.
• The initiative positions India as a leader in developing sustainable and economical access to space.
• Further tests and development are planned to scale up the RLV for operational missions, including Gaganyaan.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator) is a previous ISRO project.
Reusable launch vehicles aim to reduce launch costs significantly.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's future space endeavors and its global standing in space exploration.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of developing reusable rockets compared to conventional expendable launch systems.
Evaluate the challenges ISRO faces in perfecting RLV technology and integrating it into its operational launch schedule.
Examine how advancements in space technology like RLVs contribute to national development and security.
GS3.imageDescription: An artist's impression of ISRO's reusable launch vehicle prototype descending for a controlled landing.
GS3.trivia: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is currently the world's only orbital-class reusable rocket.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Development' 2025
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled the 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Development 2025'.
• The policy aims to foster green infrastructure, efficient resource management, and resilient urban planning across Indian cities.
• Key pillars include promoting renewable energy, improving waste management, enhancing public transport, and conserving urban biodiversity.
• It emphasizes citizen participation and the adoption of smart city solutions for better governance and quality of life.
• The policy sets ambitious targets for reducing carbon footprint and achieving climate resilience in urban areas by 2035.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry for urban development.
Sustainable Urban Development focuses on environmental, social, and economic sustainability.
Smart Cities Mission is an existing government initiative for urban transformation.
India is committed to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical components of the National Policy on Sustainable Urban Development and its potential to transform Indian cities.
Discuss the challenges in implementing sustainable urban policies, including funding, governance, and public awareness.
Evaluate the role of technology and citizen participation in achieving the goals of sustainable urban development.
Examine how this policy aligns with India's international commitments on climate change and sustainable development.
GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic green city with vertical gardens, solar panels, and integrated public transport.
GS3.trivia: By 2050, it is estimated that over 50% of India's population will live in urban areas.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Export Promotion Scheme for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs)
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched a dedicated 'MSME Export Promotion Scheme' to boost their global outreach.
• The scheme offers financial incentives, capacity building, and market access support to MSMEs looking to export their products.
• It aims to integrate Indian MSMEs into global value chains and increase their contribution to India's total exports.
• Key components include subsidies for international trade fairs, quality certification, and digital marketing platforms.
• This initiative is expected to create new employment opportunities and strengthen India's position in global trade.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: MSMEs are defined based on investment in plant and machinery/equipment and turnover.
The Ministry of MSME and Ministry of Commerce and Industry are involved in MSME promotion.
Exports contribute significantly to India's GDP and foreign exchange reserves.
India aims to become a global manufacturing hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of MSMEs in India's economic growth, employment generation, and export performance.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian MSMEs in accessing global markets and how the new scheme addresses them.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in promoting MSME exports and integrating them into global supply chains.
Suggest additional measures to enhance the competitiveness and resilience of India's MSME sector.
GS3.imageDescription: A small factory worker inspecting products, with shipping containers in the background.
GS3.trivia: MSMEs account for nearly 30% of India's GDP and 45% of its manufacturing output.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Mega Railway Electrification Project Worth ₹50,000 Crore
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to a massive railway electrification project valued at ₹50,000 crore.
• The project aims to electrify an additional 10,000 route kilometers of Indian Railways network by 2030.
• This initiative is a major step towards reducing carbon emissions, enhancing energy efficiency, and modernizing rail infrastructure.
• It will facilitate faster and more reliable train operations, benefiting both freight and passenger services.
• The project is expected to generate significant employment opportunities and boost the domestic manufacturing sector.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Indian Railways is one of the world's largest railway networks.
Electrification aims to reduce dependence on fossil fuels.
The project aligns with India's net-zero targets.
Union Cabinet is the highest decision-making body in the executive branch.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic and environmental benefits of large-scale railway electrification projects in India.
Analyze the challenges in executing mega infrastructure projects, including land acquisition, funding, and technological integration.
Evaluate the role of Indian Railways in national development and its contribution to achieving sustainable development goals.
Examine how modernization of railway infrastructure can enhance India's logistics capabilities and boost economic growth.
GS3.imageDescription: An electric locomotive pulling a passenger train through a scenic Indian landscape.
GS3.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world by size.

GS3.headline: CERT-In Issues High-Alert Cybersecurity Advisory Amidst Surge in Phishing Attacks
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued a high-priority cybersecurity advisory.
• The advisory warns against a significant surge in sophisticated phishing and ransomware attacks targeting Indian users and organizations.
• It provides detailed recommendations for individuals and enterprises to safeguard their digital assets and sensitive information.
• CERT-In emphasized the importance of strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, and regular software updates.
• The agency urged immediate reporting of any suspicious activity to mitigate potential financial losses and data breaches.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Phishing is a type of cyber attack where malicious actors impersonate trusted entities.
Ransomware encrypts data and demands payment for its release.
The Information Technology Act, 2000, governs cybercrime in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India, particularly phishing and ransomware, and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the role of government agencies like CERT-In in enhancing India's cybersecurity posture and protecting critical infrastructure.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current cybersecurity policies and legal frameworks in combating cybercrime.
Suggest measures to improve public awareness and digital literacy to mitigate the risks of cyber attacks.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of a lock icon over a circuit board, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

GS3.headline: New Agricultural Drone Policy to Boost Productivity and Address Labor Shortages
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new policy promoting the use of drones in agriculture.
• The policy aims to enhance farm productivity, optimize resource utilization, and address the growing challenge of labor shortages.
• Drones will be utilized for precision spraying of fertilizers and pesticides, crop health monitoring, and land surveying.
• The government will provide subsidies and regulatory support for farmers and Agri-startups adopting drone technology.
• This initiative is part of a broader push to modernize Indian agriculture and improve farmers' income.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Drones are Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs).
Precision agriculture uses technology to optimize crop yields and resource use.
The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is the nodal ministry.
India's drone rules were liberalized in 2021.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of drone technology to revolutionize Indian agriculture and address persistent challenges like low productivity and labor scarcity.
Analyze the policy framework supporting drone adoption in agriculture, including subsidies and regulatory aspects.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of agricultural drones on small and marginal farmers, and potential issues like digital divide.
Examine how integrating technology into farming aligns with the goal of doubling farmers' income and achieving food security.
GS3.imageDescription: An agricultural drone spraying pesticides over a field of crops.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat.

GS3.headline: India Inaugurates Asia's Largest Floating Solar Energy Park in Gujarat
GS3.explainer:
• India has inaugurated Asia's largest floating solar energy park, located on the Sardar Sarovar Dam reservoir in Gujarat.
• The mega project boasts a capacity of 600 MW, significantly boosting India's renewable energy generation.
• Floating solar panels offer advantages like reduced land use, lower evaporation rates, and higher efficiency due to water cooling.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and combating climate change.
• The project is expected to inspire similar installations across the country, leveraging India's vast water bodies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Sardar Sarovar Dam is on the Narmada River in Gujarat.
Floating solar panels are an emerging technology for renewable energy.
India aims to achieve 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
Gujarat is a leading state in renewable energy generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the advantages and challenges of floating solar energy parks compared to conventional ground-mounted solar installations.
Analyze the role of renewable energy projects like this in India's energy transition and its commitment to climate action.
Evaluate the technological advancements and policy support needed to scale up floating solar capacity across the country.
Examine the environmental implications of large-scale floating solar projects on aquatic ecosystems and water quality.
GS3.imageDescription: Vast arrays of solar panels floating on a large reservoir, reflecting the sky.
GS3.trivia: The world's largest floating solar plant is currently in China.

sources: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
